Problem: Insufficient thrust output relative to engine weight limiting maneuverability and climb performance
Solution: Optimize compressor efficiency and reduce engine weight through advanced composite materials
Data needed: Thrust output, Engine weight, Compressor efficiency data
Data Source: Engine test bed data, Simulation output, Flight telemetry
Sensors/Tech: Load cells for thrust measurement, Weight sensors, Temperature sensors
Formula/Explanation: T/W = Thrust (N) / Engine Weight (N). Higher ratios indicate better performance. Improved by increasing thrust or reducing weight through material optimization.
Visualization: Gauge chart, Line chart with historical trends

Problem: High fuel consumption per unit thrust reducing range and increasing operational costs
Solution: Optimize combustion efficiency and increase bypass ratio for improved fuel economy
Data needed: Fuel flow rate, Thrust output, Air-fuel ratio, Combustion temperature
Data Source: Fuel flow sensors, Engine test data, CFD simulation
Sensors/Tech: Fuel flow meters, Mass flow sensors, Thermocouples
Formula/Explanation: SFC = Fuel Consumption (mg/s) / Thrust (N). Lower values indicate better efficiency. Optimized through improved combustion and higher bypass ratios.
Visualization: Numeric indicator with % change, Line chart

Problem: Low thermal efficiency resulting in poor energy conversion and fuel wastage
Solution: Enhance thermal management systems and optimize aerodynamic blade design
Data needed: Thermodynamic model data, CFD analysis, Component efficiency metrics
Data Source: CFD + test data, Performance analysis software, Thermodynamic models
Sensors/Tech: Temperature sensors, Pressure sensors, Flow meters
Formula/Explanation: η = (Useful Work Output / Heat Input) × 100. Combines combustion, turbine, and nozzle efficiency. Improved through thermal management and aerodynamic optimization.
Visualization: Gauge chart, Progress bar with % change vs target
Frequency: Per flight cycle, Real-time during operation

Problem: Frequent unplanned downtime reducing operational readiness and mission capability
Solution: Implement AI-driven predictive maintenance and improve component reliability
Data needed: Uptime data, Downtime records, Failure logs, Maintenance schedules
Data Source: Maintenance management system, Digital twin, Historical operational data
Sensors/Tech: IoT sensors, Condition monitoring systems, SCADA
Formula/Explanation: Availability = (Uptime / Total Time) × 100. Improved through predictive maintenance scheduling and high-quality components.
Visualization: Progress bar, KPI card with uptime metrics

Problem: Unpredictable component failure leading to costly unplanned maintenance
Solution: Deploy predictive models using digital twin and historical degradation data
Data needed: Fatigue data, Thermal cycling records, Vibration data, Operating hours
Data Source: Predictive maintenance models, Digital twin analytics, Historical data
Sensors/Tech: Vibration sensors, Acoustic emission sensors, Temperature monitoring
Formula/Explanation: RUL = f(thermal cycles, operating hours, vibration levels, maintenance quality). Predicted using ML models analyzing degradation patterns.
Visualization: Color-coded numeric indicator, Trend chart showing degradation

Problem: Excessive temperature causing material degradation and reduced component life
Solution: Enhance cooling air flow and apply advanced thermal barrier coatings
Data needed: Temperature sensor data, Cooling flow rates, Material thermal properties
Data Source: Temperature sensors, Test bed telemetry, CFD thermal analysis
Sensors/Tech: High-temperature thermocouples, Pyrometers, IR sensors
Formula/Explanation: TIT measured directly at turbine inlet. Indicates thermal loading. Managed through cooling optimization and TBC application.
Visualization: Thermometer-style indicator, Trend line chart
Frequency: Real-time continuous, Per second during operation

Problem: Suboptimal bypass air-to-core air ratio affecting thrust and efficiency balance
Solution: Optimize fan blade design and adjust fan diameter to achieve target BPR
Data needed: Bypass airflow, Core airflow, Fan design parameters
Data Source: CFD analysis, Engine design data, Test instrumentation
Sensors/Tech: Mass flow sensors, Pitot tubes, Pressure sensors
Formula/Explanation: BPR = Bypass Air Mass Flow / Core Air Mass Flow. For military turbofans, low BPR (0.3–0.6) balances thrust and efficiency.
Visualization: Dial gauge, Numeric display

Problem: Frequent component failures reducing reliability and increasing maintenance burden
Solution: Improve component quality and implement scheduled preventive maintenance
Data needed: Failure event logs, Operating hours between failures, Component degradation data
Data Source: Maintenance logs, Reliability database, Operational history
Sensors/Tech: Health monitoring sensors, Diagnostic systems
Formula/Explanation: MTBF = Total Operating Time / Number of Failures. Higher values indicate better reliability. Improved through quality components and preventive maintenance.
Visualization: Bar chart, Trend line showing reliability improvement

Problem: Insufficient compression reducing combustion efficiency and thrust output
Solution: Increase compressor stages or improve compressor blade aerodynamics
Data needed: Inlet pressure, Outlet pressure, Compressor performance data
Data Source: Pressure sensors, Compressor test data
Sensors/Tech: Static and dynamic pressure sensors, Transducers
Formula/Explanation: OPR = Compressor Outlet Pressure / Inlet Pressure. Higher ratios improve thermal efficiency. Optimized through blade design and stage count.
Visualization: Numeric display, Line chart

Problem: Inadequate thrust without afterburner limiting subsonic performance
Solution: Optimize turbine efficiency and increase mass flow rate through engine
Data needed: Thrust measurement, Turbine inlet temperature, Mass flow rate
Data Source: Load cells, Test stand data, Flight telemetry
Sensors/Tech: Thrust load cells, Strain gauges
Formula/Explanation: Dry Thrust = Mass Flow Rate × (Exit Velocity - Inlet Velocity). Optimized through turbine efficiency and mass flow improvements.
Visualization: Bar chart, Gauge indicator

Problem: Insufficient augmented thrust limiting supersonic capability and acceleration
Solution: Optimize afterburner fuel injection and nozzle geometry for efficient combustion
Data needed: Afterburner fuel flow, Nozzle area, Thrust with reheat
Data Source: Thrust sensors, Fuel flow meters, Nozzle position sensors
Sensors/Tech: Load cells, Fuel flow sensors, Position encoders
Formula/Explanation: Reheat Thrust = Dry Thrust + Additional Thrust from Afterburner. Optimized through fuel-air mixing and nozzle control.
Visualization: Bar chart comparing dry vs reheat, Gauge
Frequency: Real-time during reheat operation, Test analysis

Problem: Excessive weight reducing aircraft performance and payload capacity
Solution: Utilize lightweight advanced materials (titanium alloys
Data needed: composites) and topology optimization
Data Source: Component weights, Material densities, Structural requirements
Sensors/Tech: CAD models, Weight measurements, Material specifications
Formula/Explanation: Precision scales, Weight sensors
Units: Total Engine Weight = Σ(Component Weights). Reduced through material selection and structural optimization without compromising strength.
Visualization: kg (kilograms)

Problem: Excessive stress at turbine blade root fillet approaching yield strength causing crack initiation risk
Solution: Redesign fillet geometry to increase radius and upgrade to higher-strength material
Data needed: Structural load data, Material properties, FEA mesh results, Boundary conditions
Data Source: FEA software output, Stress analysis models, Test validation data
Sensors/Tech: Strain gauges for validation, Digital image correlation (DIC)
Formula/Explanation: Von Mises Stress = √[(σ₁-σ₂)² + (σ₂-σ₃)² + (σ₃-σ₁)²]/√2. Indicates equivalent stress combining all principal stresses. Design improvement needed when approaching yield.
Visualization: Heat map on 3D model, Contour plot, Trend chart

Problem: High strain at compressor blade leading edge indicating potential plastic deformation and low cycle fatigue
Solution: Install interblade damping system and increase local wall thickness at critical regions
Data needed: Displacement field data, Strain measurements, Material elastic properties
Data Source: FEA displacement analysis, Strain gauge measurements
Sensors/Tech: Strain gauges, Digital image correlation systems
Formula/Explanation: Principal Strain = ΔL/L₀ (change in length / original length). Maximum value indicates critical deformation zones. Managed through geometry optimization and damping.
Visualization: Strain contour map, Line chart trend
Frequency: Per design cycle, Validation with physical gauges

Problem: Components operating too close to material failure limits risking structural failure
Solution: Reduce operating loads or upgrade to materials with higher yield strength
Data needed: Actual stress values, Material yield strength, Safety factor requirements
Data Source: FEA stress analysis, Material property database
Sensors/Tech: Stress sensors, Material testing equipment
Formula/Explanation: Stress-to-Yield Ratio = (Actual Stress / Yield Strength) × 100. Values above 80% require immediate attention. Safety factors applied for design margins.
Visualization: Bar chart by component, Heat map
Frequency: Per analysis, Real-time monitoring for critical parts

Problem: Accelerated fatigue damage accumulation reducing component service life below target
Solution: Apply shot peening for residual compressive stress and install oxidation-resistant coatings
Data needed: Cyclic load history, S-N curve data, Mean stress, Stress amplitude
Data Source: Fatigue analysis software, Historical load data, Material fatigue curves
Sensors/Tech: Cycle counters, Load sensors
Formula/Explanation: Fatigue Life (N) = f(stress amplitude, mean stress, material S-N curve). Predicted using Goodman or Soderberg criteria. Extended through surface treatments.
Visualization: Progress bar showing % remaining, Prediction chart

Problem: Insufficient margin between actual thermal stress and allowable limits causing thermal fatigue
Solution: Optimize cooling passages and apply advanced thermal barrier coatings (TBC)
Data needed: Temperature distribution, Thermal expansion coefficients, Constraint conditions
Data Source: CFD thermal analysis, Thermal FEA, Temperature sensors
Sensors/Tech: Thermocouples, IR thermal cameras
Formula/Explanation: Thermal Stress = E × α × ΔT × (constraint factor). Margin = (Allowable - Actual)/Allowable × 100. Improved through cooling design and TBC application.
Visualization: Gauge chart, Thermal contour map
Frequency: Per thermal cycle, Real-time during operation

Problem: Excessive structural deflection indicating insufficient stiffness and potential interference issues
Solution: Increase component thickness or add structural ribs to enhance stiffness
Data needed: Displacement field from FEA, Boundary conditions, Load cases
Data Source: FEA displacement analysis, Deflection measurements
Sensors/Tech: Laser displacement sensors, LVDTs
Formula/Explanation: Maximum Displacement = max(|u|) where u is displacement vector field. Indicates structural flexibility. Controlled through geometry optimization and stiffening.
Visualization: Deformation contour plot, Vector field visualization
Frequency: Per load case analysis, Design verification

Problem: High overall system failure risk due to combination of severity and poor detection
Solution: Deploy advanced monitoring (AI thermal imaging
Data needed: acoustic emission) and improve filtration systems
Data Source: Severity ratings (1-10), Occurrence ratings (1-10), Detection ratings (1-10)
Sensors/Tech: FMEA worksheets, Failure history database, Expert assessments
Formula/Explanation: Health monitoring sensors, Diagnostic equipment
Units: RPN = Severity × Occurrence × Detection. Values > 100 require mitigation actions. Reduced through improved detection and occurrence reduction.
Visualization: dimensionless
Frequency: Heat map by component, Bar chart ranked by RPN
Threshold: Per FMEA review cycle, Quarterly updates

Problem: Multiple high-severity failure modes threatening system safety and reliability
Solution: Implement design modifications and enhanced monitoring for critical failure modes
Data needed: List of failure modes, Severity classifications, Current mitigations
Data Source: FMEA database, Historical failure records
Sensors/Tech: Continuous monitoring systems, Inspection tools
Formula/Explanation: Count of failure modes with Severity ≥ 8. Each critical mode requires dedicated mitigation strategy and monitoring.
Visualization: Bar chart by subsystem, Priority matrix

Problem: High risk of blade structural failure due to prolonged high-temperature exposure
Solution: Upgrade to single-crystal superalloy and implement real-time thermal monitoring
Data needed: Temperature exposure data, Material creep properties, Cooling effectiveness
Data Source: Temperature sensors, Material degradation models, Test data
Sensors/Tech: High-temperature thermocouples, Thermal cameras
Formula/Explanation: RPN = S(8) × O(4) × D(5) = 160. Reduced by improving detection (thermal monitoring) and occurrence (material upgrade).
Visualization: RPN trend chart, Mitigation impact visualization

Problem: High risk of high-cycle fatigue cracks due to resonant vibration
Solution: Conduct modal analysis and apply shot peening to reduce stress concentration
Data needed: Vibration frequency data, Stress amplitude, Blade natural frequencies
Data Source: Vibration sensors, FEA modal analysis, Strain measurements
Sensors/Tech: Accelerometers, Strain gauges, Eddy current sensors
Formula/Explanation: RPN = S(9) × O(5) × D(3) = 135. Mitigated through vibration damping and surface treatment to extend fatigue life.
Visualization: RPN comparison chart, Trend analysis

Problem: Risk of bearing failure due to lubrication contamination and wear
Solution: Install high-efficiency filtration and deploy AI oil analysis for contamination detection
Data needed: Oil debris particle count, Vibration signatures, Temperature data
Data Source: Oil analysis lab, Vibration sensors, Temperature monitors
Sensors/Tech: Oil debris sensors, Acoustic emission sensors
Formula/Explanation: RPN = S(7) × O(6) × D(2) = 84. Reduced by improving detection (acoustic monitoring, AI analysis) and lowering occurrence (better filtration).
Visualization: RPN trend, Mitigation timeline

Problem: Risk of combustion instability and hot spots due to fuel system contamination
Solution: Implement inline filtration and AI-based fuel flow monitoring
Data needed: Fuel flow rate, Pressure drop across nozzle, Combustion temperature uniformity
Data Source: Fuel flow sensors, Pressure transducers, Thermal imaging
Sensors/Tech: Flow meters, Pressure sensors, Particulate monitors
Formula/Explanation: RPN = S(7) × O(7) × D(3) = 147. Mitigated through filtration (occurrence reduction) and AI flow pattern analysis (detection improvement).
Visualization: RPN bar chart, Flow uniformity map

Problem: Current thrust below target affecting aircraft performance envelope
Solution: Increase compressor pressure ratio and optimize turbine blade twist angles
Data needed: Target thrust specification, Current thrust measurement, Compressor parameters
Data Source: Thrust test stand, Performance simulation, CFD analysis
Sensors/Tech: Load cells, Pressure sensors
Formula/Explanation: Thrust = ṁ × (V_exit - V_inlet) + (P_exit - P_ambient) × A_exit. Optimized through compressor and turbine design iterations.
Visualization: Bar chart target vs actual, Optimization convergence plot

Problem: SFC above target reducing range and increasing fuel costs
Solution: Increase turbine inlet temperature and optimize combustor fuel-air mixing
Data needed: Fuel consumption rate, Thrust output, TIT, Combustor efficiency
Data Source: Fuel system sensors, Performance models, CFD combustion analysis
Sensors/Tech: Fuel flow meters, Temperature sensors
Formula/Explanation: SFC = Fuel Flow Rate / Thrust. Lower is better. Optimized through TIT increase (within material limits) and combustor efficiency improvement.
Visualization: Line chart showing optimization progress, Target comparison
Frequency: Per optimization cycle, Flight test validation

Problem: Predicted component fatigue life significantly below target due to high stress
Solution: Redesign critical features (increase fillet radius) and apply surface treatments (shot peening)
Data needed: Stress amplitude data, Mean stress, Material S-N curves, Geometry parameters
Data Source: FEA fatigue analysis, Material testing, Design models
Sensors/Tech: Fatigue test rigs, Stress analyzers
Formula/Explanation: Fatigue Life = f(stress range, mean stress, surface finish, material properties). Extended through geometry optimization and surface enhancement.
Visualization: Progress bar vs target, Optimization parameter influence chart

Problem: Peak stress exceeds material safety limits requiring design modification
Solution: Apply topology optimization and consider material upgrade to higher-yield alloys
Data needed: Stress distribution from FEA, Material yield strength, Load cases
Data Source: FEA structural analysis, Optimization algorithms
Sensors/Tech: Strain gauges for validation
Formula/Explanation: Max Stress from FEA. Reduced through topology optimization (redistributing material) and material selection (higher yield strength).
Visualization: Heat map on model, Optimization iteration trend

Problem: Overall design not meeting multi-objective targets (performance + constraints)
Solution: Run multi-objective optimization balancing thrust
Data needed: efficiency
Data Source: stress
Sensors/Tech: and fatigue life
Formula/Explanation: All performance KPIs, Constraint satisfaction metrics, Design variables
Units: Optimization software, Performance database, Constraint checkers
Visualization: All performance and structural monitoring systems
Frequency: Composite Score = Weighted Sum of (Performance Gains + Constraint Satisfaction + Convergence + Multi-objective Balance). Maximized through iterative optimization.
column13: Spider/radar chart showing multiple objectives, Progress gauge
column14: Per major design iteration, Weekly optimization runs
